assertions.reason: `hostname` executed successfully on `shuvtest` via `@shuvdex/ssh`, returning stdout `shuvtest`, exitCode 0, and an `ssh.executeCommand` span. Stderr only contained the known post-quantum SSH warning.

`echo stdout-capture && nonexistent_command_shuvdex_validator_007` produced a `CommandFailed` result with stdout `stdout-capture`, stderr containing the shell `command not found` message, and exitCode 127, confirming output and exit-code capture.
Connecting to RFC 5737 address `192.0.2.1` with `timeoutMs: 3000` failed as a typed `ConnectionTimeout` after 3008 ms, demonstrating connect-timeout enforcement distinct from command runtime.

`whoami` on `shuvtest` completed successfully in 293 ms with exitCode 0 and no password/passphrase prompt text observed, indicating non-interactive key-based authentication. Stderr only contained the known post-quantum SSH warning.

`exit 1` and a nonexistent command both surfaced as `CommandFailed` with exit codes 1 and 127, while the unreachable host surfaced as `ConnectionTimeout`, confirming command failures are distinguished from connection failures.

frictions.description: Every successful SSH connection to `shuvtest` emitted the known post-quantum key exchange warning on stderr.
frictions.resolution: Recorded the warning in evidence and ignored it for pass/fail, per mission guidance.

summary: Tested 5 SSH foundation assertions through the built package API: 5 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked.
